pattern: Picking the middle element as the root of each slice keeps the two sides equal in size, so a sorted array becomes a height-balanced BST.
hints: The root should split the array so neither side is much taller than the other — the middle is the natural choice.
Recurse on the left half for the left subtree and the right half for the right subtree, each time taking that half's middle.
Any consistent middle (e.g. the lower middle of lo..hi) yields a valid height-balanced BST; the in-order reading is just the original array.

edge_inputs.description: even length so the middle is ambiguous
power-of-two-minus-one length builds a perfect tree

stress.description: random sorted array of distinct values
stress.note: sorted ascending as the problem requires
